---
description: Verified Traefik v3.6 → v3.7.8 upgrade facts for the itsUP proxy pin — CVE fixes, the v3.7.7 Host(*) catch-all matcher change affecting the generated noop router, BasicAuth non-empty requirement, and the absence of plugin/file-provider breaking changes.
---

# Traefik v3.7 — Upgrade Notes (v3.6.x → v3.7.8)

Curated from the official Traefik migration guide (`doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v3/`)
and the v3.7.8 release notes (read 2026-07-21), for the itsUP proxy image pin.

## Fixes carried

- v3.7.8 addresses advisory GHSA-8rxv-jg7p-wvg3; v3.7.7 and v3.6.23 each
  patched further CVEs. Staying on the latest v3.7.x carries all of them.
- WebSocket retry-middleware crash fix, certificate-resolver log-noise fix.

## Behavior changes relevant to itsUP

- **`Host(*)` is a catch-all since v3.7.7.** Previously it matched only
  single-segment hosts; now it matches every request including hostless ones.
  itsUP's generated `routers-http.yml` uses `Host(`*`)` on the `web` noop
  router — under v3.7.7+ it matches all unrouted traffic on `web`. Specific
  host routers keep priority (longer rules outrank the short catch-all), so
  routing of declared ingress is unaffected; the noop router simply becomes a
  true catch-all.
- **BasicAuth requires non-empty users since v3.7.3** — an empty-user
  basicauth unmounts its routers (404) instead of answering 401. itsUP's
  `traefik-auth` middleware is rendered from the required `TRAEFIK_ADMIN`
  secret (generation fails without it), so the config is always non-empty.
- **`UnderscoreHeadersStrategy`** entrypoint option added (v3.7.6), default
  `keep` — no behavior change unless opted in.

## Not affected

- No changes to `experimental.plugins` loading, file-provider dynamic
  configuration, or entrypoint `http.middlewares` semantics between v3.6 and
  v3.7 — the itsUP generation contract (static config + dynamic files +
  docker labels) is unchanged.
- Kubernetes-only changes (Gateway API/CRD updates) do not apply.

## Sources

- https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v3/ (official migration guide)
- https://github.com/traefik/traefik/releases/tag/v3.7.8
